question_title: What will be the price of Ethereum (ETH) on July 10, 2026?
summary: ETH trades ~$1,622 on June 29, 2026 (bounced from a June 25 low of ~$1,565). Forecasting the July 10 close is ~11 days out. Crypto behaves approximately as a martingale, so I center on the current price with a mild downward bias from the ongoing altcoin-weakness / "Bitcoin season" regime. Empirical daily volatility: ~2.1% over the last 14 days, ~3.5% over the full crash-contaminated month — I blended to ~2.5–3% with fat tails (Student-t mixture across vol regimes). Monte Carlo (300k paths) gives median ~$1,607, P10 ~$1,423, P90 ~$1,810. I deliberately did NOT use the contaminated -0.73%/day drift (dominated by the early-June crash); drift set near zero. Bounds are open so tails extend modestly beyond.

factors.description: Current ETH ~$1,622, random-walk martingale anchor for short horizon
Empirical vol: 14-day ~2.1%, full-period ~3.5%; blended 2.5-3% with fat tails sets distribution width
Altcoin weakness / Bitcoin-season narrative imparts slight downward bias

reasoning:
Forecast submitted. 

**Summary:** ETH is at ~$1,622 on June 29, 2026. For the July 10 close (~11 days out), I treated crypto as an approximate martingale and ran a Monte Carlo (300k paths) with empirical volatility blended to ~2.5–3% daily (Student-t fat tails, mixed across vol regimes), near-zero drift, and a mild downward bias from the altcoin-weakness regime. I avoided the crash-contaminated -0.73%/day drift from the early-June window.

**Distribution:** median ~$1,607, P10 ~$1,423, P90 ~$1,810, with open tails extending to ~$1,243 (P1) and ~$2,061 (P99).
sources_consulted: [Ethereum USD (ETH-USD) Price History & Historical Data - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ETH-USD/history/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the closing price in US dollars of Ethereum (ETH) for the calendar date July 10, 2026 (UTC), as reported in the "Close" column of the CoinGecko Ethereum historical data table: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/ethereum/historical_data — for example, a close of $1,650.42 resolves as 1650.42. The value is expressed in US dollars per ETH.
fine_print: The relevant figure is the row dated 2026-07-10 in CoinGecko's ETH historical data table (USD). If CoinGecko has not published a Close value for that date, the resolver should use the ETH/USD price shown on the CoinGecko Ethereum page (https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/ethereum) as of 00:00 UTC on July 11, 2026, or the value reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) for ETH/USD at that time. If CoinGecko permanently changes its methodology or becomes unavailable, the question resolves based on the spot ETH/USD price reported by credible sources for the July 10, 2026 (UTC) daily close.

factors.description: Current ETH price ~$1,622 (June 29), random walk anchor for 11-day horizon
Recent 14-day daily vol ~2.1%, full-period ~3.5% (includes crash); blended ~2.5-3% for forward sim
Mild altcoin weakness / Bitcoin season narrative gives slight downward bias

input.anchor: Random walk from current $1,622 with empirical daily vol ~2.5-3% over 11 days
input.assessment: ETH at ~$1,622 on June 29, 2026. Forecasting July 10 close (11 days out). Crypto ~martingale, so center on current price with slight downward bias from altcoin weakness. Empirical daily vol blended ~2.5-3% with fat tails (Student-t). Monte Carlo mixture of vol regimes gives P10~1423, median~1607, P90~1810. Width consistent with random-walk over 11 days (3%*sqrt(11)~10% one-sigma). Bounds open so tails extend beyond.
input.tool_audit: web_search gave current price and 30-day history (Yahoo recent_history) — sufficient. execute_code ran Monte Carlo cleanly.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward short-horizon crypto price question. Empirical vol + Monte Carlo is the right approach; tools worked smoothly. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Clean numeric forecast. I verified each factor against the trace and ran the consistency checks.

FACTOR GROUNDING (all confirmed):
- Factor 1 (current ~$1,622): Yahoo api_data in trace shows current_price 1622.52 and June 29 close 1622.52. Grounded. logit=0 consistent with random-walk anchor.
- Factor 2 (14-day vol ~2.1%, full-period ~3.5%): execute_code output shows last14 std 0.02073 (2.07%) and all std 0.03492 (3.49%). Exact match. "Includes crash" is supported — the data window starts at $2,004 (May 31) and crashes to ~$1,565 by June 6 (~-22%). Grounded.
- Factor 3 (altcoin weakness / Bitcoin season, slight downward bias, logit=-0.2): Supported by trace's -5.2% 10-day trailing return and by worldview ETH/BTC research (structural downtrend since Aug 2025, ~33% decline). Negative logit sign correctly matches "downward bias."

REGIME-SPANNING DATA WINDOW (the key risk for this question type): The agent computed a contaminated full-period drift of -0.73%/day from a window straddling the early-June crash. Crucially, it did NOT use this drift — both Monte Carlo runs set mu=0.0 / -0.0005 with the explicit note that drift is "dominated by early crash, use ~0 drift," and it blended vol down to 2.5-3% rather than the contaminated 3.5%. The agent correctly neutralized the regime break instead of letting it pollute the estimate. Good practice, not an error.

PROBABILITY/DISTRIBUTION: My independent estimate matches the agent's. At ~3%/day over 11 days, one-sigma ≈ 10% (~$160). Median ~$1,607-1,614 with mild downward bias is justified; P10~1423 / P90~1810 follows directly from the Monte Carlo and is well-calibrated. No meaningful divergence.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Genuine — constructs a concrete alternative (macro/BTC liquidation cascade below $1,500 toward $1,400, or ETF catalyst above $1,800), cites the recent low ($1,564), and flags that the blended 2.5-3% vol may understate fat tails relative to the 3.5% full-period figure. Not a token gesture.

RESOLUTION CRITERIA: Fully provided (CoinGecko ETH Close for 2026-07-10 UTC). Factors and center/range engage with the correct quantity (ETH/USD daily close).

CONFIDENCE / TOOL AUDIT: Self-confidence 0.70 is appropriately humble. Tool-audit narrative is consistent with ground-truth metrics (5 calls, 0 errors); no false "no failures" claim. Worldview ETH entries (June 2, stale/superseded) corroborate rather than contradict the factors.

No hallucination, double-counting, wrong-direction factor, or contradictory assessment found.
